content.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
content.summary: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, NFTs, Web3 and crypto regulation.

content.title: UK FCA seeks feedback on guidance for crypto rules ahead of 2027 rollout
content.summary: The UK financial regulator has launched a consultation on crypto rules covering stablecoins, trading and staking ahead of a broader UK crypto regime expected to take effect in 2027.

content.title: Tom Lee says ‘mini crypto winter’ is over, sees Ether above $60K
content.summary: Bitmine chairman Tom Lee told Paris Blockchain Week that the recent crypto slump was a “mini crypto winter” and said Ether could climb above $60,000 over the next few years.

content.title: WLFI proposes unlock plan for 62 billion tokens with long lockups and burns
content.summary: The new proposal outlines multi-year lockups and opt-in token burn, as the Trump-linked DeFi platform responds to pressure over delayed liquidity access.

content.title: Only 4% of Danish citizens hold crypto, far below other European countries
content.summary: Only 4% of Danish citizens own crypto, far below other European countries, as banks, taxes and risk fears limit adoption, according to a new staff paper from the country's central bank.

content.title: Fireblocks launches tool for institutions to earn yield on stablecoins
content.summary: Fireblocks launched Earn, giving institutions direct access to Aave and Morpho-based stablecoin lending as firms seek yield on idle balances.

content.title: Virginia updates law to hold unclaimed crypto in-kind for at least one year
content.summary: Virginia signed a law bringing digital assets into unclaimed property rules, requiring in-kind transfer and limiting how quickly the state can sell them.

content.title: Pakistan allows banks to serve licensed crypto firms after years-long ban
content.summary: Pakistan’s central bank has allowed banks to open accounts for licensed virtual asset service providers, ending years of restrictions and marking a shift toward a regulated framework.

content.title: EU adviser says ‘MiCA 2’ is likely as crypto market matures: PBW 2026
content.summary: EU officials plan to reassess MiCA as companies test its limits, with industry feedback set to shape potential changes to the bloc’s crypto framework.

content.title: OKX rolls out X-Perps across Europe in regulated derivatives push
content.summary: The exchange said the new product is available across the European Economic Area through its Malta-based MiFID business, with up to 10x leverage and multi-asset collateral.
